Autoplot methods for gradepath frontier objects

Source: R/plots-frontier.R
autoplot.gp_frontier.Rd

ggplot2 ggplot2::autoplot() methods so autoplot(fr) (a gp_frontier), autoplot(fit) (a gp_fit), or autoplot(path) (a gp_grade_path) draw the information-reliability frontier via gp_plot_frontier() without attaching ggplot2.

Usage

# S3 method for class 'gp_frontier'
autoplot(object, ...)

# S3 method for class 'gp_fit'
autoplot(object, ...)

# S3 method for class 'gp_grade_path'
autoplot(object, ...)

Arguments

object

A gp_frontier, gp_fit, or gp_grade_path.

...

Further arguments forwarded to gp_plot_frontier().

Value

A ggplot object (class c("gg", "ggplot")); it renders only when printed.

See also

gp_plot_frontier(), gp_frontier()

Other gradepath-plots: autoplot.gp_report_card(), gp_plot_discordance(), gp_plot_frontier(), gp_plot_posterior_contrast(), gp_plot_report_card(), scale_color_gradepath(), theme_gradepath()

Examples

fit <- readRDS(system.file("extdata/examples/tiny_fit.rds", package = "gradepath"))
autoplot(fit)   # the information-reliability frontier for a gp_fit

On this page
